title: Badge Access Audit

index=physical sourcetype="access_control"
| table _time, badge_holder, badge_id, door, action, result

description: Complete badge access audit trail is required for compliance (SOC2, PCI-DSS) and supports security investigations.
value: Complete badge access audit trail is required for compliance (SOC2, PCI-DSS) and supports security investigations.
implementation: Forward access control events to Splunk. Parse all badge events (granted, denied, door held, forced). Retain per compliance requirements. Enable search by person, door, or time for investigations.

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Access control syslog/API.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Access control system events.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Forward access control events to Splunk. Parse all badge events (granted, denied, door held, forced). Retain per compliance requirements. Enable search by person, door, or time for investigations.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="access_control"
| table _time, badge_holder, badge_id, door, action, result
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Badge Access Audit** — Complete badge access audit trail is required for compliance (SOC2, PCI-DSS) and supports security investigations.

Documented **Data sources**: Access control system events. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Access control syslog/API. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: physical; **sourcetype**: access_control. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=physical, sourcetype="access_control". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Pipeline stage (see **Badge Access Audit**): table _time, badge_holder, badge_id, door, action, result
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (access log), Bar chart (access by door), Timeline (access events for person), Single value (total access today).
grandmaExplanation: Complete badge access audit trail is required for compliance (SOC2, ) and supports security investigations — so power, cooling, and physical access problems are found before they hurt uptime.
